# GAUSS Table Creator / pubtable

## Project Summary

This repo modernizes an outdated GAUSS table creator into `pubtable`, a GAUSS-first library for publication-quality tables from GAUSS results, summary statistics, matrices, and custom data. Legacy code exists only as compatibility material; new work should prefer the modern `pt` API.

Reference repos:

- Existing table library: https://github.com/aptech/gauss_table_creator
- GAUSS coding/reference repo: https://github.com/ec78/gauss-programming-llm
- Local GAUSS reference repo, if present: `C:\Users\eclow\Documents\GitHub\gauss-programming-llm`

## Target Users

GAUSS users who need publication-ready regression/model tables, summary-statistics tables, matrix/data tables, and exports to common writing/reporting formats.

## Core Principles

- GAUSS-first syntax and idioms.
- Simple composable procedures, not heavy abstractions.
- Separate table objects, model adapters, formatting, rendering, and exporting.
- Explicit adapters for known GAUSS output structs; use `isStructType()` only for safe routing.
- MVP-first implementation with tests/examples/docs updated together.
- Preserve legacy code only where it helps migration or maintainability.
- Do not invent GAUSS functions; verify against GAUSS docs, examples, or the reference repo.

## Public API Direction

Current/provisional modern API:

- `ptTableCreate(title)`
- `ptTableFromMatrix(x, rowNames, colNames, title)`
- `ptTableFrom(out)` for `isStructType()` dispatch
- `ptModelCreate(name, estimates, stdErrors)`
- `ptModelFrom(name, out)` for typed model adapters
- `ptModelSetCI(model, ciLower, ciUpper)`, `ptModelSetStatRows(model, statRows)`, `ptSetStatRows(tbl, statRows)`, `ptModelSetNotes(model, notes)`
- `ptModelTable(model)`
- `ptModelCompare(models)` aligns models on the union of term/GOF names, blanking missing cells
- `ptModelCompareWith(models, opts)` adds term/GOF ordering, coefficient renaming, and notes via `ptCompareOptions`
- `ptExport(tbl, fname)`

`statRows` controls which statistic rows render under each coefficient: `se`, `tstat`, `pvalue`, `ci` (default `"se"`). `ci` requires `ptModelSetCI` first.

`ptCompareOptions` (built with `ptCompareOptionsCreate`, configured via `ptCompareSetTermOrder`, `ptCompareSetGofOrder`, `ptCompareSetLabelMap`, `ptCompareSetNotes`, `ptCompareSetColGroups`) lets `ptModelCompareWith` reorder term/GOF rows, rename term labels for display, append table-level notes alongside per-model notes, and add grouped/spanning column headers over the comparison columns (e.g. for equation/quantile/multi-model panels).

`ptSetColGroups(tbl, colGroups)` sets one column-group label per body column (blank for ungrouped columns); contiguous equal labels form a span, rendered as a pseudo-span in Markdown/CSV/Text, `\multicolumn`/`\cmidrule` in LaTeX, `colspan` in HTML, and `\clmgf`/`\clmrg` cell merges in RTF.

`ptSetColAlign(tbl, colAlign)` / `ptModelSetColAlign(model, colAlign)` (one `l`/`c`/`r` per column including the stub) now drives alignment in every renderer: Markdown gets a matching alignment row (`:---`/`:---:`/`---:`), HTML cells get `style="text-align:..."`, plain text columns are left/center/right-padded, and LaTeX uses the string as the `tabular` column spec. Leaving `colAlign` unset keeps the previous defaults (stub left, data right).

`ptExportAll(tables, fname)` writes an array of `ptTable` structs to one file, dispatching on extension like `ptExport`: text-based formats (Markdown/CSV/Text/HTML) concatenate each table's rendered output, RTF merges them into a single document, and XLS/XLSX write each table to its own sheet.

`ptExportAllFormats(tables, basename, exts)` batch-exports the same `ptTable` array to multiple formats in one call, writing `basename + "." + ext` via `ptExportAll` for each extension in `exts` (e.g. `"md" $| "tex" $| "html"`). Returns `0` if every format succeeded, otherwise the return code of the first failed format (remaining formats are still attempted).

`ptSetColFormat(tbl, colDigits)` reformats already-rendered numeric cells column by column to a chosen decimal-digit count (one entry per body column, `""` leaves a column unchanged); it re-parses each cell with `strtof` and reapplies `ptFormatNumber`, so it works on plain numeric cells but not cells already wrapped with significance stars or statistic parentheses.

`ptSetCellStyle(tbl, row, col, style)` marks a body cell (1-based) with `"bold"`, `"italic"`, `"bold italic"`, or `""`, stored in `ptTable.cellStyle`. Markdown, LaTeX, HTML, and RTF renderers apply the styling; CSV and plain text renderers ignore it.

`ptModelSetStars(model, cutoffs, symbols)` / `ptSetStars(tbl, cutoffs, symbols)` set custom significance cutoffs/symbols (default `0.10`/`0.05`/`0.01` -> `"+"`/`"*"`/`"**"`); `ptModelNoStars(model)` / `ptNoStars(tbl)` disable stars.

`ptModelApplyPreset(model, preset)` / `ptApplyPreset(tbl, preset)` apply a bundle of `ptFormat` settings for one of `"journal"`, `"journal_booktabs"`, `"compact"`, `"plain"`, or `"report"` (digits, significance stars, statRows, and statistic wrapper). `"journal_booktabs"` is identical to `"journal"` except it also sets `ptFormat.ruleStyle = "booktabs"`, which makes `ptRenderHtml`/`ptRenderRtf` draw only a table-top rule, a header-bottom rule, and a table-bottom rule (no vertical/column-divider rules); `ptRenderLatex` is unaffected since it is already booktabs-style by default, and Markdown has no border concept to affect. `ptFormat.preset` records which preset (if any) was last applied.

Exporting or rendering (`ptExport`, `ptRenderLatex`, `ptRenderHtml`, `ptRenderRtf`) a `"journal"`/`"journal_booktabs"` table with an empty `title` prints a non-fatal `errorlog` warning ("pubtable warning: journal-style table has no title. Use ptSetTitle(tbl, ...) before exporting.") via the shared helper `_ptCheckJournalTitle`, but still completes the export/render.

`ptModelSetDataLabel(model, label)` sets `ptModel.dataLabel`, rendered by `ptModelTable`/`ptModelCompareWith` as a separate `"Data: <label>."` note, kept apart from `ptModelSetNotes`/`ptModelSetGOF` content so callers don't have to hand-concatenate a dataset description into another note string.

`ptModelSetAicBic(model, tf)` toggles whether a model's *optional* trailing AIC/BIC GOF rows are shown (default off). `ptModelFromCmlmt`/`ptModelFromMaxlikmt` always compute and append AIC/BIC as the last two `gofNames`/`gofValues` entries (`AIC = -2*fval + 2*k`, `BIC = -2*fval + 2*k*ln(n)`) and set `ptModel.hasOptionalAicBic = 1`; `ptFilterGofRows` (called from `ptModelTable`/`ptModelCompareWith`) drops exactly those last two rows by *position* when `hasOptionalAicBic` is set and `showAicBic` is not. This never matches on the row label text, so it does not affect adapters that already report their own non-optional `"AIC"`/`"BIC"` GOF rows (e.g. `ptModelFromGlm`), since those never set `hasOptionalAicBic`.

Plain-text and Markdown rendering (`ptRenderText`, `ptRenderMarkdown`) right-pad each data cell with `ptApplyStarGutter`/`ptTrailingDecorLen` so a coefficient's significance-star suffix (0/1/2 characters) and its stat sub-row's wrapping punctuation (`)`/`]`) consume the same fixed-width trailing slot before column-width padding is computed — without this, a coefficient's number and its SE/CI row's number would land in different columns whenever the star count differs from row to row.

Built-in adapters (always available with `library pubtable;`):

- `olsmtOut`
- `glmOut`
- `gmmOut` through `ptModelFrom`
- `dstatmtOut`
- `fglsOut`

`pubtableSet()` detects installed optional GAUSS libraries at runtime (via `fopen(getGAUSSHome()...)`) and writes `pubtable.dec` with `declare matrix _pubtable_ver = {0, 3, 0};` plus `#define PT_USE_X` entries for each found library (cmlmt, maxlikmt, optmt, tsmt). `ptSetup()` is an alias for `pubtableSet()`; `ptSetupAt(srcDir)` writes `pubtable.dec` to a specified directory for dev/git-clone installs. `src/pubtable.dec` ships as a template with just the version declaration; the generated version is machine-specific. `_ptSetupSilent()` runs as top-level code at the end of `pubtable.src` — on first load of an installed package it silently auto-generates `pubtable.dec` (checks for "Auto-generated" via `fgets`; only proceeds if `pkgs/pubtable/src/pubtable.src` exists); the file then takes effect on the next include.

Each `PT_USE_X`-gated adapter file (`pubtable_cmlmt.src`, `pubtable_maxlikmt.src`, `pubtable_optmt.src`, `pubtable_tsmt.src`) includes `pubtable.dec` itself (right after `#include pubtable.sdf`), so user programs never need to `#include` anything — just load the optional library together with pubtable in a single statement: `library cmlmt, pubtable;`. Loading them as two separate `library` statements is wrong, since each statement unloads every previously loaded library not named in it. qardl uses `QARDL_SDF_INCLUDED` (set by `qardl.sdf`'s own include guard) instead of a `pubtable.dec` entry, with the same `library qardl, pubtable;` loading pattern.

Each adapter proc is declared once with variadic `(...)` args and fetches its typed arguments via `dynargsGet()` inside an inner `#ifDef PT_USE_X / #else / #endIf` (or `#ifDef QARDL_SDF_INCLUDED` for qardl) — not two duplicated proc bodies. The `#else` branch calls `_library_missing_error(currentprocname(), "LibName")`, which is defined unconditionally in `pubtable.src` itself (not in any adapter file, to avoid structure-conflict errors from library-specific `.sdf` types). Adapter `.src` files are NOT `#include`d from `pubtable.src` — they are listed only in `package.json`'s `"src"` array, so `library pubtable;` compiles them automatically.

Most optional add-on adapters are wired into `ptModelFrom(name, out)`, so calling `ptModelFrom` works the same way for add-on structs as for built-in ones once the library is loaded. Exceptions: `optmtResults` (no covariance matrix, so no `ptModel` form exists — use `ptTableFromOptmt`), `tscsmtOut` (two distinct estimators, no single canonical model — use `ptFromTscsmt`), and `qardlOut`/`qardlECMOut` (need an extra `tauIdx` argument — use `ptFromQardl`/`ptFromQardlECM`).

Optional add-on package adapters (separate files):

- `src/pubtable_maxlikmt.src`: `ptModelFromMaxlikmt`/`ptFromMaxlikmt` for `maxlikmtResults`. Load with `library maxlikmt, pubtable;`.
- `src/pubtable_cmlmt.src`: `ptModelFromCmlmt`/`ptFromCmlmt` for `cmlmtResults`. Load with `library cmlmt, pubtable;`.
- `src/pubtable_tsmt.src`: tsmt adapters (load with `library tsmt, pubtable;`; the tsPanel adapters also need `#include tspanel.src`): `ptModelFromArimamt`/`ptFromArimamt` (`arimamtOut`), `ptModelFromTsPanel`/`ptFromTsPanel` (`tsPanelEstimationOut`), `ptModelFromAutomt`/`ptFromAutomt` (`automtOut`), `ptModelFromVarmamt`/`ptFromVarmamt` (`varmamtOut`), `ptModelFromLsdvmt`/`ptFromLsdvmt` (`lsdvmtOut`), `ptModelFromSwitchmt`/`ptFromSwitchmt` (`switchmtOut`), `ptModelFromGarchmt`/`ptFromGarchmt` (`garchEstimation`), and `ptFromTscsmt` (`tscsmtOut`, a `ptModelCompare` table of within/FE vs. error-components estimates)
- `src/pubtable_optmt.src`: `ptTableFromOptmt` for `optmtResults`, a parameter/estimate/gradient table with no SE row since `optmtResults` has no covariance matrix. Load with `library optmt, pubtable;`.
- `src/pubtable_qardl.src`: QARDL package (ARDL/QARDL/NARDL/CS-ARDL family) adapters. Load with `library qardl, pubtable;`. `ptModelFromArdl`/`ptFromArdl` (`ardlOut`), `ptModelFromArdlECM`/`ptFromArdlECM` (`ardlECMOut`), `ptModelFromQardl`/`ptFromQardl` and `ptModelFromQardlECM`/`ptFromQardlECM` (`qardlOut`/`qardlECMOut`, one `ptModelCompare` column per quantile in `out.tau`), `ptModelFromNardl`/`ptFromNardl` and `ptModelFromNardlECM`/`ptFromNardlECM` (`nardlOut`/`nardlECMOut`), `ptModelFromCsardl`/`ptFromCsardl` and `ptModelFromCsardlECM`/`ptFromCsardlECM` (`csardlOut`/`csardlECMOut`), `ptFromArdlFull`/`ptTablesFromQardlFull`/`ptTablesFromNardlFull`/`ptTablesFromCsardlFull` for the `*Full` workflow outputs, and the `ptFromArdlFamily` dispatcher

Current exporters:

- Markdown `.md`
- LaTeX `.tex`
- CSV `.csv`
- plain text `.txt`
- Excel `.xls` through GAUSS `SpreadsheetWrite`; `.xlsx` is provisional/where supported
- Word-compatible RTF `.rtf` (real RTF table with grid borders and bold header row)
- HTML `.html`/`.htm`

True `.docx` is provisional/future work.

LaTeX rendering supports `ptSetLabel`/`ptModelSetLabel` (adds `\label{...}` after `\caption{...}`) and `ptSetColAlign`/`ptModelSetColAlign` (overrides the default `l` + `r...` column alignment spec; one `l`/`c`/`r` per column including the stub).

## File Structure

- `src/pubtable.sdf`: modern structs: `ptFormat`, `ptTable`, `ptModel`
- `src/pubtable.src`: format/presets, internal utilities, table struct and setters, dispatchers (`ptTableFrom`, `ptModelFrom`) and built-in adapters, setup (`pubtableSet`/`ptSetup`/`ptSetupAt`), `_library_missing_error`
- `src/pubtable_model.src`: all `ptModel*` and `ptCompare*` procedures
- `src/pubtable_render.src`: number formatting and all `ptRender*` procedures
- `src/pubtable_export.src`: all `ptExport*` and file I/O helpers
- `src/pubtable_cmlmt.src`, `src/pubtable_maxlikmt.src`, `src/pubtable_optmt.src`, `src/pubtable_tsmt.src`, `src/pubtable_qardl.src`: optional add-on library adapters; listed in `package.json`, not `#include`d from `pubtable.src`
- `src/pubtable_legacy.sdf`: legacy structs
- `src/pubtable_legacy_output.src`: legacy output helpers
- `src/pubtable_legacy_setters.src`: legacy setter helpers
- `package.json`: GAUSS package manifest — `"src"` array lists every file above; `library pubtable;` compiles them all
- `examples/`: modern and legacy examples
- `tests/test_pubtable.e`: current GAUSS smoke/source test (includes the four core split files directly; does not exercise the optional adapters)
- `README.md`: user-facing overview
- `docs/`: API reference (`docs/api/`) plus older legacy command docs
- `lib/pubtable.xml`: GAUSS application manifest (legacy installer path)

## Testing

Run:

```powershell
C:\gauss26\tgauss.exe C:\Users\eclow\Documents\GitHub\gauss_table_creator\tests\test_pubtable.e
```

Also run:

```powershell
git -C C:\Users\eclow\Documents\GitHub\gauss_table_creator diff --check
```

Note: GAUSS may print a command-log permission warning in this environment; the test pass/fail message is what matters.

## Common Development Tasks

- Add an exporter: implement `ptRender...`, route in `ptExport`, add example/test/docs.
- Add an output adapter: inspect the source `.sdf`, implement explicit `ptModelFrom...` or `ptFrom...`, add `isStructType()` dispatch, add tests.
- Improve model comparison: preserve existing API; add term alignment and tests for mismatched model terms.
- Update docs: keep README examples, `examples/`, and tests synchronized.
- Rename files carefully: avoid collisions with native GAUSS source filenames and update `package.json` plus `lib/pubtable.xml`.

## GAUSS Reminders

- GAUSS is case-insensitive.
- Prefer `sprintf` for user-facing formatting.
- Use supported string functions such as `strindx`, `strsect`, `strreplace`, and `strtof`.
- `strindx()` returns `0` when not found.
- Use `getorders()` for arrays, `rows()`/`cols()` for matrices.
- Check existing GAUSS examples before assuming a function exists.
